one_line_ask: Audit the supplied Maison Valcere luxury wordmark pack (master SVG + raster export) against the brand guideline and the press/foil spec, and return a marked-up, region-referenced vector & print-readiness findings report listing every hairline-stroke, line-weight, palette, kerning and non-print-ready-path defect and the exact rule it breaks.
brief: Maison Valcere is a fictional independent high-jewelry house (fine rings, pendants and bespoke pieces) that has just received its finished logo pack from an outside brand studio and, before it goes onto embossed presentation boxes, foil-stamped certificates, the e-commerce header and the video title cards, wants an independent set of eyes to confirm the mark is genuinely production-clean. The identity is meant to feel unmistakably premium — a deep-black canvas with crisp white typography, minimal ornamentation, purely typographic with no symbols or icons — and the house is strict that every asset stays consistent across packaging, print and screen. You are handed four things: (1) valcere_wordmark_master.svg, the "production" vector master the studio delivered; (2) valcere_wordmark_web.png, the flat raster export the house has been using online; (3) valcere_brand_guideline.pdf, the guideline stating the monochrome palette (Valcere Noir #0E0E10, Champagne #E8DCC2, Blanc #FAF8F3 — with the rule that the wordmark is "purely typographic, no symbols or icons, never recoloured outside the monochrome palette, minimum print width 22mm, minimum hairline stroke 0.35pt / 1.5px at favicon size"
